verses.latin_vulgate: Postquam autem cessavit tumultus, vocatis Paulus discipulis, et exhortatus eos, valedixit, et profectus est ut iret in Macedoniam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: μετὰ δὲ τὸ παύσασθαι τὸν θόρυβον προσκαλεσάμενος ὁ παῦλος τοὺς μαθητὰς καὶ ἀσπασάμενος ἐξῆλθεν πορευθῆναι εἰς τὴν μακεδονίαν
verses.king_james: And after the uproar was ceased, Paul called unto *him* the disciples, and embraced *them*, and departed for to go into Macedonia.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem perambulasset partes illas, et exhortatus eos fuisset multo sermone, venit ad Græciam:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διελθὼν δὲ τὰ μέρη ἐκεῖνα καὶ παρακαλέσας αὐτοὺς λόγῳ πολλῷ ἦλθεν εἰς τὴν ἑλλάδα
verses.king_james: And when he had gone over those parts, and had given them much exhortation, he came into Greece,

verses.latin_vulgate: ubi cum fecisset menses tres, factæ sunt illi insidiæ a Judæis navigaturo in Syriam: habuitque consilium ut reverteretur per Macedoniam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ποιήσας τε μῆνας τρεῖς γενομένης αὐτῷ ἐπιβουλῆς ὑπὸ τῶν ἰουδαίων μέλλοντι ἀνάγεσθαι εἰς τὴν συρίαν ἐγένετο γνώμῃ τοῦ ὑποστρέφειν διὰ μακεδονίας
verses.king_james: And *there* abode three months. And when the Jews laid wait for him, as he was about to sail into Syria, he purposed to return through Macedonia.

verses.latin_vulgate: Comitatus est autem eum Sopater Pyrrhi Berœensis, Thessalonicensium vero Aristarchus, et Secundus, et Gajus Derbeus, et Timotheus: Asiani vero Tychicus et Trophimus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: συνείπετο δὲ αὐτῷ ἄχρι τῆς ἀσίας σώπατρος βεροιαῖος θεσσαλονικέων δὲ ἀρίσταρχος καὶ σεκοῦνδος καὶ γάϊος δερβαῖος καὶ τιμόθεος ἀσιανοὶ δὲ τυχικὸς καὶ τρόφιμος
verses.king_james: And there accompanied him into Asia Sopater of Berea; and of the Thessalonians, aristarchus and Secundus; and Gaius of Derbe, and Timotheus; and of Asia, Tychicus and Trophimus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hi cum præcessissent, sustinuerunt nos Troade:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὗτοι προελθόντες ἔμενον ἡμᾶς ἐν τρῳάδι
verses.king_james: These going before tarried for us at Troas.

verses.latin_vulgate: nos vero navigavimus post dies azymorum a Philippis, et venimus ad eos Troadem in diebus quinque, ubi demorati sumus diebus septem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡμεῖς δὲ ἐξεπλεύσαμεν μετὰ τὰς ἡμέρας τῶν ἀζύμων ἀπὸ φιλίππων καὶ ἤλθομεν πρὸς αὐτοὺς εἰς τὴν τρῳάδα ἄχρις ἡμερῶν πέντε οὐ διετρίψαμεν ἡμέρας ἑπτά
verses.king_james: And we sailed away from Philippi after the days of unleavened bread, and came unto them to Troas in five days; where we abode seven days.

verses.latin_vulgate: Una autem sabbati cum convenissemus ad frangendum panem, Paulus disputabat cum eis profecturus in crastinum, protraxitque sermonem usque in mediam noctem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐν δὲ τῇ μιᾷ τῶν σαββάτων συνηγμένων τῶν μαθητῶν τοῦ κλάσαι ἄρτον ὁ παῦλος διελέγετο αὐτοῖς μέλλων ἐξιέναι τῇ ἐπαύριον παρέτεινέν τε τὸν λόγον μέχρι μεσονυκτίου
verses.king_james: And upon the first *day* of the week, when the disciples came together to break bread, Paul preached unto them, ready to depart on the morrow; and continued his speech until midnight.

verses.latin_vulgate: Erant autem lampades copiosæ in cœnaculo, ubi eramus congregati.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἦσαν δὲ λαμπάδες ἱκαναὶ ἐν τῷ ὑπερῴῳ οὗ ἦσαν συνηγμένοι
verses.king_james: And there were many lights in the upper chamber, where they were gathered together.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sedens autem quidam adolescens nomine Eutychus super fenestram, cum mergeretur somno gravi, disputante diu Paulo, ductus somno cecidit de tertio cœnaculo deorsum, et sublatus est mortuus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καθήμενος δέ τις νεανίας ὀνόματι εὔτυχος ἐπὶ τῆς θυρίδος καταφερόμενος ὕπνῳ βαθεῖ διαλεγομένου τοῦ παύλου ἐπὶ πλεῖον κατενεχθεὶς ἀπὸ τοῦ ὕπνου ἔπεσεν ἀπὸ τοῦ τριστέγου κάτω καὶ ἤρθη νεκρός
verses.king_james: And there sat in a window a certain young man named Eutychus, being fallen into a deep sleep: and as Paul was long preaching, he sunk down with sleep, and fell down from the third loft, and was taken up dead.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad quem cum descendisset Paulus, incubuit super eum: et complexus dixit: Nolite turbari, anima enim ipsius in ipso est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καταβὰς δὲ ὁ παῦλος ἐπέπεσεν αὐτῷ καὶ συμπεριλαβὼν εἶπεν μὴ θορυβεῖσθε ἡ γὰρ ψυχὴ αὐτοῦ ἐν αὐτῷ ἐστιν
verses.king_james: And Paul went down, and fell on him, and embracing *him* said, Trouble not yourselves; for his life is in him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ascendens autem, frangensque panem, et gustans, satisque allocutus usque in lucem, sic profectus est.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀναβὰς δὲ καὶ κλάσας ἄρτον καὶ γευσάμενος ἐφ᾽ ἱκανόν τε ὁμιλήσας ἄχρις αὐγῆς οὕτως ἐξῆλθεν
verses.king_james: When he therefore was come up again, and had broken bread, and eaten, and talked a long while, even till break of day, so he departed.

verses.latin_vulgate: Adduxerunt autem puerum viventem, et consolati sunt non minime.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἤγαγον δὲ τὸν παῖδα ζῶντα καὶ παρεκλήθησαν οὐ μετρίως
verses.king_james: And they brought the young man alive, and were not a little comforted.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nos autem ascendentes navem, navigavimus in Asson, inde suscepturi Paulum: sic enim disposuerat ipse per terram iter facturus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἡμεῖς δὲ προελθόντες ἐπὶ τὸ πλοῖον ἀνήχθημεν εἲς τὴν Ἆσσον ἐκεῖθεν μέλλοντες ἀναλαμβάνειν τὸν παῦλον οὕτως γὰρ ἦν διατεταγμένος μέλλων αὐτὸς πεζεύειν
verses.king_james: And we went before to ship, and sailed unto Assos, there intending to take in Paul: for so had he appointed, minding himself to go afoot.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem convenisset nos in Asson, assumpto eo, venimus Mitylenen.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ συνέβαλεν ἡμῖν εἰς τὴν Ἆσσον ἀναλαβόντες αὐτὸν ἤλθομεν εἰς μιτυλήνην
verses.king_james: And when he met with us at Assos, we took him in, and came to Mitylene.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et inde navigantes, sequenti die venimus contra Chium, et alia applicuimus Samum, et sequenti die venimus Miletum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: κἀκεῖθεν ἀποπλεύσαντες τῇ ἐπιούσῃ κατηντήσαμεν ἀντικρύ χίου τῇ δὲ ἑτέρᾳ παρεβάλομεν εἰς σάμον καὶ μεὶναντες ἐν τρωγυλλίῳ, τῇ ἐχομένῃ ἤλθομεν εἰς μίλητον
verses.king_james: And we sailed thence, and came the next *day* over against Chios; and the next *day* we arrived at Samos, and tarried at Trogyllium; and the next *day* we came to Miletus.

verses.latin_vulgate: Proposuerat enim Paulus transnavigare Ephesum, ne qua mora illi fieret in Asia. Festinabat enim, si possibile sibi esset, ut diem Pentecostes faceret Jerosolymis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔκρινεν γὰρ ὁ παῦλος παραπλεῦσαι τὴν ἔφεσον ὅπως μὴ γένηται αὐτῷ χρονοτριβῆσαι ἐν τῇ ἀσίᾳ ἔσπευδεν γὰρ εἰ δυνατὸν ἦν αὐτῷ τὴν ἡμέραν τῆς πεντηκοστῆς γενέσθαι εἰς ἱεροσόλυμα
verses.king_james: For Paul had determined to sail by Ephesus, because he would not spend the time in Asia: for he hasted, if it were possible for him, to be at Jerusalem the day of Pentecost.

verses.latin_vulgate: A Mileto autem mittens Ephesum, vocavit majores natu ecclesiæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀπὸ δὲ τῆς μιλήτου πέμψας εἰς ἔφεσον μετεκαλέσατο τοὺς πρεσβυτέρους τῆς ἐκκλησίας
verses.king_james: And from Miletus he sent to Ephesus, and called the elders of the church.

verses.latin_vulgate: Qui cum venissent ad eum, et simul essent, dixit eis: Vos scitis a prima die qua ingressus sum in Asiam, qualiter vobiscum per omne tempus fuerim,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς δὲ παρεγένοντο πρὸς αὐτὸν εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὑμεῖς ἐπίστασθε ἀπὸ πρώτης ἡμέρας ἀφ᾽ ἡς ἐπέβην εἰς τὴν ἀσίαν πῶς μεθ᾽ ὑμῶν τὸν πάντα χρόνον ἐγενόμην
verses.king_james: And when they were come to him, he said unto them, Ye know, from the first day that I came into Asia, after what manner I have been with you at all seasons,

verses.latin_vulgate: serviens Domino cum omni humilitate, et lacrimis, et tentationibus, quæ mihi acciderunt ex insidiis Judæorum:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δουλεύων τῷ κυρίῳ μετὰ πάσης ταπεινοφροσύνης καὶ πολλῶν δακρύων καὶ πειρασμῶν τῶν συμβάντων μοι ἐν ταῖς ἐπιβουλαῖς τῶν ἰουδαίων
verses.king_james: Serving the Lord with all humility of mind, and with many tears, and temptations, which befell me by the lying in wait of the Jews:

verses.latin_vulgate: quomodo nihil subtraxerim utilium, quominus annuntiarem vobis et docerem vos, publice et per domos,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὡς οὐδὲν ὑπεστειλάμην τῶν συμφερόντων τοῦ μὴ ἀναγγεῖλαι ὑμῖν καὶ διδάξαι ὑμᾶς δημοσίᾳ καὶ κατ᾽ οἴκους
verses.king_james: *And* how I kept back nothing that was profitable *unto you*, but have shewed you, and have taught you publickly, and from house to house,

verses.latin_vulgate: testificans Judæis atque gentilibus in Deum pœnitentiam, et fidem in Dominum nostrum Jesum Christum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διαμαρτυρόμενος ἰουδαίοις τε καὶ ἕλλησιν τὴν εἰς τὸν θεὸν μετάνοιαν καὶ πίστιν τὴν εἰς τὸν κύριον ἡμῶν ἰησοῦν χριστόν
verses.king_james: Testifying both to the Jews, and also to the Greeks, repentance toward God, and faith toward our Lord Jesus Christ.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc ecce alligatus ego spiritu, vado in Jerusalem: quæ in ea ventura sint mihi, ignorans:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ νῦν ἰδού, ἐγὼ δεδεμένος τῷ πνεύματι πορεύομαι εἰς ἰερουσαλήμ τὰ ἐν αὐτῇ συναντήσοντά μοι μὴ εἰδώς
verses.king_james: And now, behold, I go bound in the spirit unto Jerusalem, not knowing the things that shall befall me there:

verses.latin_vulgate: nisi quod Spiritus Sanctus per omnes civitates mihi protestatur, dicens quoniam vincula et tribulationes Jerosolymis me manent.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πλὴν ὅτι τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον κατὰ πόλιν διαμαρτύρεταί λέγον ὅτι δεσμὰ με καὶ θλίψεις μένουσιν
verses.king_james: Save that the Holy Ghost witnesseth in every city, saying that bonds and afflictions abide me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sed nihil horum vereor: nec facio animam meam pretiosiorem quam me, dummodo consummem cursum meum, et ministerium verbi quod accepi a Domino Jesu, testificari Evangelium gratiæ Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀλλ᾽ οὐδενὸς λόγον ποιοῦμαι οὐδὲ ἔχω τὴν ψυχὴν μου τιμίαν ἐμαυτῷ ὡς τελειῶσαι τὸν δρόμον μου μετὰ χαρᾶς, καὶ τὴν διακονίαν ἣν ἔλαβον παρὰ τοῦ κυρίου ἰησοῦ διαμαρτύρασθαι τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τῆς χάριτος τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: But none of these things move me, neither count I my life dear unto myself, so that I might finish my course with joy, and the ministry, which I have received of the Lord Jesus, to testify the gospel of the grace of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc ecce ego scio quia amplius non videbitis faciem meam vos omnes, per quos transivi prædicans regnum Dei.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ νῦν ἰδού, ἐγὼ οἶδα ὅτι οὐκέτι ὄψεσθε τὸ πρόσωπόν μου ὑμεῖς πάντες ἐν οἷς διῆλθον κηρύσσων τὴν βασιλείαν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: And now, behold, I know that ye all, among whom I have gone preaching the kingdom of God, shall see my face no more.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quapropter contestor vos hodierna die, quia mundus sum a sanguine omnium.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ μαρτύρομαι ὑμῖν ἐν τῇ σήμερον ἡμέρᾳ ὅτι καθαρός ἐγὼ ἀπὸ τοῦ αἵματος πάντων
verses.king_james: Wherefore I take you to record this day, that I *am* pure from the blood of all *men*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Non enim subterfugi, quominus annuntiarem omne consilium Dei vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐ γὰρ ὑπεστειλάμην τοῦ μὴ ἀναγγεῖλαι ὑμῖν πᾶσαν τὴν βουλὴν τοῦ θεοῦ
verses.king_james: For I have not shunned to declare unto you all the counsel of God.

verses.latin_vulgate: Attendite vobis, et universo gregi, in quo vos Spiritus Sanctus posuit episcopos regere ecclesiam Dei, quam acquisivit sanguine suo.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: προσέχετε οὖν ἑαυτοῖς καὶ παντὶ τῷ ποιμνίῳ ἐν ᾧ ὑμᾶς τὸ πνεῦμα τὸ ἅγιον ἔθετο ἐπισκόπους ποιμαίνειν τὴν ἐκκλησίαν τοῦ θεοῦ ἣν περιεποιήσατο διὰ τοῦ ἰδίου αἵματος
verses.king_james: Take heed therefore unto yourselves, and to all the flock, over the which the Holy Ghost hath made you overseers, to feed the church of God, which he hath purchased with his own blood.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego scio quoniam intrabunt post discessionem meam lupi rapaces in vos, non parcentes gregi.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐγὼ γὰρ, οἶδα τοῦτο, ὅτι εἰσελεύσονται μετὰ τὴν ἄφιξίν μου λύκοι βαρεῖς εἰς ὑμᾶς μὴ φειδόμενοι τοῦ ποιμνίου
verses.king_james: For I know this, that after my departing shall grievous wolves enter in among you, not sparing the flock.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ex vobisipsis exsurgent viri loquentes perversa, ut abducant discipulos post se.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξ ὑμῶν αὐτῶν ἀναστήσονται ἄνδρες λαλοῦντες διεστραμμένα τοῦ ἀποσπᾶν τοὺς μαθητὰς ὀπίσω αὐτῶν
verses.king_james: Also of your own selves shall men arise, speaking perverse things, to draw away disciples after them.

verses.latin_vulgate: Propter quod vigilate, memoria retinentes quoniam per triennium nocte et die non cessavi, cum lacrimis monens unumquemque vestrum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: διὸ γρηγορεῖτε μνημονεύοντες ὅτι τριετίαν νύκτα καὶ ἡμέραν οὐκ ἐπαυσάμην μετὰ δακρύων νουθετῶν ἕνα ἕκαστον
verses.king_james: Therefore watch, and remember, that by the space of three years I ceased not to warn every one night and day with tears.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et nunc commendo vos Deo, et verbo gratiæ ipsius, qui potens est ædificare, et dare hæreditatem in sanctificatis omnibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ τανῦν παρατίθεμαι ὑμᾶς ἀδελφοὶ, τῷ θεῷ καὶ τῷ λόγῳ τῆς χάριτος αὐτοῦ τῷ δυναμένῳ ἐποἰκοδομῆσαι, καὶ δοῦναι ὑμῖν κληρονομίαν ἐν τοῖς ἡγιασμένοις πᾶσιν
verses.king_james: And now, brethren, I commend you to God, and to the word of his grace, which is able to build you up, and to give you an inheritance among all them which are sanctified.

verses.latin_vulgate: Argentum, et aurum, aut vestem nullius concupivi, sicut
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἀργυρίου ἢ χρυσίου ἢ ἱματισμοῦ οὐδενὸς ἐπεθύμησα
verses.king_james: I have coveted no man's silver, or gold, or apparel.

verses.latin_vulgate: ipsi scitis: quoniam ad ea quæ mihi opus erant, et his qui mecum sunt, ministraverunt manus istæ.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτοὶ δὲ γινώσκετε ὅτι ταῖς χρείαις μου καὶ τοῖς οὖσιν μετ᾽ ἐμοῦ ὑπηρέτησαν αἱ χεῖρες αὗται
verses.king_james: Yea, ye yourselves know, that these hands have ministered unto my necessities, and to them that were with me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Omnia ostendi vobis, quoniam sic laborantes, oportet suscipere infirmos ac meminisse verbi Domini Jesu: quoniam ipse dixit: Beatius est magis dare, quam accipere.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πάντα ὑπέδειξα ὑμῖν ὅτι οὕτως κοπιῶντας δεῖ ἀντιλαμβάνεσθαι τῶν ἀσθενούντων μνημονεύειν τε τῶν λόγων τοῦ κυρίου ἰησοῦ ὅτι αὐτὸς εἶπεν μακάριόν ἐστιν διδόναι μᾶλλον ἢ λαμβάνειν
verses.king_james: I have shewed you all things, how that so labouring ye ought to support the weak, and to remember the words of the Lord Jesus, how he said, It is more blessed to give than to receive.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum hæc dixisset, positis genibus suis oravit cum omnibus illis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ταῦτα εἰπὼν θεὶς τὰ γόνατα αὐτοῦ σὺν πᾶσιν αὐτοῖς προσηύξατο
verses.king_james: And when he had thus spoken, he kneeled down, and prayed with them all.

verses.latin_vulgate: Magnus autem fletus factus est omnium: et procumbentes super collum Pauli, osculabantur eum,
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἱκανὸς δὲ ἐγένετο κλαυθμὸς πάντων καὶ ἐπιπεσόντες ἐπὶ τὸν τράχηλον τοῦ παύλου κατεφίλουν αὐτόν
verses.king_james: And they all wept sore, and fell on Paul's neck, and kissed him,

verses.latin_vulgate: dolentes maxime in verbo quod dixerat, quoniam amplius faciem ejus non essent visuri. Et deducebant eum ad navem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὀδυνώμενοι μάλιστα ἐπὶ τῷ λόγῳ ᾧ εἰρήκει ὅτι οὐκέτι μέλλουσιν τὸ πρόσωπον αὐτοῦ θεωρεῖν προέπεμπον δὲ αὐτὸν εἰς τὸ πλοῖον
verses.king_james: Sorrowing most of all for the words which he spake, that they should see his face no more. And they accompanied him unto the ship.
